claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: discount messaging — "save fifteen percent on processing" — converts about three times better than feature messaging — "fast settlement, brazilian compliance built in"
claims.object_value: ~3x better conversion for discount messaging over feature messaging

claims.subject: HelixPay SEA
claims.evidence: sea data shows the opposite — feature messaging wins there
claims.object_value: feature messaging

claims.subject: HelixPay
claims.evidence: forty percent up year on year is the line in the all-hands deck
claims.object_value: 40% up year on year

claims.subject: CRM migration
claims.evidence: hubspot in brasil is still being set up because we haven't done the crm migration here yet
claims.object_value: not yet completed as of interview date

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: wendy ships something in english and it takes us three weeks to localize
claims.object_value: three weeks

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: that tells me the brasil market right now is pricing-sensitive in a way that isn't reflected in our positioning
claims.object_value: Brasil market is pricing-sensitive, not reflected in current positioning

claims.object_value: save fifteen percent on processing

claims.object_value: fast settlement, brazilian compliance built in

claims.evidence: we're running essentially two different positioning strategies in the two geos
claims.object_value: Two different positioning strategies: discount-led in Brasil, feature-led in SEA

claims.subject: Fernanda Lima

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil marketing

claims.object_value: Brasil team owns paid media fully

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil marketing
claims.evidence: content we either translate from wendy's stuff or we produce locally

claims.subject: HelixPay global brand
claims.evidence: the global brand sits with rajesh and we localize

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil marketing
claims.evidence: the friction is when something global ships and the brasil version isn't ready — that's happened a couple of times and it makes us look out of step
claims.object_value: Global content ships before Brasil localization is ready, creating out-of-step appearance

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil marketing
claims.evidence: faster than the current monthly review
claims.object_value: Monthly

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil marketing
claims.evidence: i'd want a feedback loop from sales to marketing on lead quality that's faster than the current monthly review. if maria and joão's team flagged in real time which leads are worth talking to and which aren't we could adjust paid faster
claims.object_value: Real-time feedback loop from sales to marketing on lead quality to enable faster paid adjustments

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil marketing
claims.evidence: i'd want a content agent that could keep our portuguese content in sync with the global content
claims.object_value: Content agent to keep Portuguese content in sync with global English content

claims.subject: HelixPay Brasil
claims.evidence: sometimes the local version drifts from the global brand voice
claims.object_value: Local Portuguese content sometimes drifts from global brand voice

claims.subject: HelixPay
claims.evidence: the lead volume number gets celebrated and the lead quality story gets a footnote. that's the gap. forty percent up year on year is the line in the all-hands deck and the conversion-to-paid number doesn't get the same airtime
claims.object_value: Lead volume (40% YoY) celebrated; conversion-to-paid metric underreported in all-hands
